Manning Passing Academy
recap.

What we're chasing in frame, what we're not, and what the finished recap should feel like. Postgame handles all filming and production at the camp and delivers two finished assets — one vertical, one horizontal — ready for Cane's to post.

Peyton + Eli

Assets · 9:16 + 16:9
The Thesis

Not a highlight reel.
The moments between the reps.

Thirty years of the same thing: legends, hands-on with kids. The drills aren't the story — the Mannings with the campers are. Candid reactions, the small human moments most cameras miss. We shoot what it actually felt like to be there.

The Approach

Mic'd up.
Up close.

We put audio on Peyton and Eli and embed videographers right in the action — close enough to catch the coaching, the one-liners, the one-on-ones with campers. From that single day we cut one horizontal recap and one vertical recap, both built from the same footage.

Reference frames throughout are placeholders for now — we'll drop in stills and clips as we lock the look.

The Day

How the day unfolds.

The beats we're chasing, start to finish. Each one is a thread in the recap — and a stack of vertical moments along the way.

Beat 01 — Morning
Camp Wakes Up
The field filling up — warmups, first drills, the energy before it ramps. Setting the scene.
Beat 02 — On the Field
The Mannings Coaching
Peyton and Eli running drills, hands-on with campers. The reason everyone's here.
Beat 03 — Mic'd Up
Heard, Not Just Seen
The audio off Peyton and Eli — coaching cues, jokes, real-time reactions. The layer that makes it personal.
Beat 04 — One-on-One
The Kids
Campers getting direct reps with legends. Faces, effort, the moment it clicks. Close on reactions.
Beat 05 — Where Cane's Lives
In the Environment
The brand present at camp — in the environment, not staged into it. Logo on the actual mark, never typed on.
Beat 06 — Close of Day
Last Light
Wind-down, goodbyes, the field emptying out. The natural close of the recap.
